solution.p: To get rid of fractions, we multiply each term in this equation by the common denominator, which is {7r\mathopen{}\left(r+2\right)} in this example.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} \amp {\frac{1}{r+2}+\frac{2}{r^{2}+2r}} = \frac{1}{7} \\ \amp {\frac{1}{r+2}+\frac{2}{r\mathopen{}\left(r+2\right)}} =\frac{1}{7} \\ \amp {\frac{1}{r+2}\cdot 7r\mathopen{}\left(r+2\right)+\frac{2}{r\mathopen{}\left(r+2\right)}\cdot 7r\mathopen{}\left(r+2\right)} = \frac{1}{7} \cdot 7 r (r+2) \\ \amp {7r+14} = {r^{2}+2r} \\ \amp {7r+14-r^{2}-2r} = 0 \\ \amp {5r-r^{2}+14} = 0 \\ \amp {-1\mathopen{}\left(5r-r^{2}+14\right)} = (-1) \cdot 0 \\ \amp {r^{2}-5r-14} = 0 \\ \amp {\left(r+2\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-7\right)} = 0 \\ \amp r+2=0 \:\: or\:\: r - 7=0 \\ \amp r=-2 \:\: or\:\: r=7 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: We must check solutions when we solve rational equations. In the original equation, both denominators cannot be 0. We set them to 0, so we can find out what values to exclude from the solution set.
We must exclude 0 and -2 from the solution set. We can see r=-2 is an extraneous solution, so the final solution is r=7.
